item.text: I’m treating pass 3 as a fresh residual-risk audit, not a replay of pass 1/2. I’m checking the story-00 guardrails the design explicitly calls out now: absolute root resolution, git-repo membership, and unreadable prompt-insert handling, plus whether those cases are actually verified.

1058	## Security and Operational Guardrails
  1059	
  1060	This runtime reads project files and launches external CLIs. The first version still needs basic guardrails:
  1061	
  1062	| Guardrail | Rule |
  1063	|---|---|
  1064	| Spec-pack root | resolve to an absolute path and require it to be inside a git repo for all commands, including `inspect` |
  1065	| Story/session continuation | reject mismatched `storyId` / `sessionId` reuse |
  1066	| Prompt inserts | treat as trusted project artifacts, but cap file size and reject unreadable paths |
  1067	| Child-process cwd | always use the resolved repo root or explicit project root |
  1068	| Timeouts | every provider adapter call has a bounded timeout |
  1069	| Secrets in logs | redact obvious bearer tokens / auth strings before persisting stdout/stderr artifacts |
  1070

  1072

item.text: I found two real story-00 guardrail gaps: we weren’t enforcing the git-repo requirement, and unreadable prompt inserts were being treated like “absent” instead of an explicit invalid-insert failure. I’m fixing those now and adding focused guardrail tests, including one to prove `inspect` returns an absolute root even when invoked with a relative path.

item.text:
**findings fixed (id, summary, files changed)**  
- `pass3-F1`: `inspect` did not enforce the Story 0 guardrail that the spec-pack root must live inside a git repo. Added repo-root detection and a blocking result for outside-repo spec packs. Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/core/git-repo.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/core/spec-pack.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/tests/security-guardrails.test.ts`  
- `pass3-F2`: unreadable public prompt inserts were being treated like “absent,” which masked an invalid project artifact. `inspect` now blocks with `PROMPT_INSERT_INVALID` when an insert file exists but is unreadable. Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/core/fs-utils.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/core/spec-pack.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/commands/inspect.ts`, `processes/impl-cli/tests/security-guardrails.test.ts`  
- `pass3-F3`: absolute spec-pack root resolution was implemented but not verified. Added a guardrail test that invokes `inspect` with a relative path and asserts the returned root is absolute. Files changed: `processes/impl-cli/tests/security-guardrails.test.ts`

**findings surfaced to orchestrator (id, summary, why uncertain)**  
- []

**files changed in this pass**  
- `processes/impl-cli/core/fs-utils.ts`  
- `processes/impl-cli/core/git-repo.ts`  
- `processes/impl-cli/core/spec-pack.ts`  
- `processes/impl-cli/commands/inspect.ts`  
- `processes/impl-cli/tests/security-guardrails.test.ts`

**gate result after this pass (pass / fail / not-run)**  
- `pass`

- Reason: added one new `security-guardrails.test.ts` file with three legitimate Story 0 guardrail tests for absolute root resolution, git-repo enforcement, and unreadable prompt-insert handling.
